data.title: SOUN Stock Looks Weak on Technical Charts — Buy or Wait?
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) continues to stay on investors' radar as a familiar name in the AI space. While the company is growing its presence in voice AI and connected cars, sentiment around the stock is mixed. Short-term technical indicators remain weak, suggesting some investors may wait for clearer momentum before buying. At the same time, analysts remain optimistic, pointing to SoundHound's voice AI growth as a potential driver of upside in the months ahead.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound AI specializes in voice recognition and natural language processing, offering AI-driven solutions across industries.

SOUN Stock Technical Analysis

According to TipRanks' technical analysis tool, SOUN is showing bearish momentum. The stock is trading at $10.78, below its 50-day exponential moving average (EMA) of $11.96, which signals near-term weakness. The broader moving-average outlook also leans negative, with the technical consensus flashing a Strong Sell rating.

This suggests that SOUN's short-term trend lacks strength, suggesting investors may want to stay cautious and wait for clearer technical confirmation before stepping in.

Overall, the stock currently carries 14 bearish signals, alongside four neutral and four bullish indicators.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Gold Price Could Rise 10% by Year-End and Hit $5,400 an Ounce, Says Goldman Sachs (GS)
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) raised its year-end gold price forecast by more than 10%, pointing to steady demand from private investors on top of strong buying by central banks and ETFs. The bank lifted its December 2026 gold target to $5,400 an ounce, up from a prior call of $4,900, according to a note dated January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goldman said the higher forecast assumes that private investors who bought gold as a hedge against policy risks will hold on to those positions through the end of the year.

Why Goldman Is More Bullish on Gold

Goldman said today's gold buying is not tied to one-off events like elections. Instead, investors are using gold to guard against longer-term risks, such as high debt levels and unclear policy direction. Because these risks are unlikely to ease soon, the bank expects investors to hold their gold positions for longer.

Gold prices have already jumped more than 70% over the past year and continue to hit new highs in early 2026. The rise reflects strong demand for safe assets as global tensions grow and concerns increase about the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Goldman also sees strong support from central banks, which are expected to buy about 60 tons of gold per month in 2026, mainly from emerging markets. In addition, Western gold ETFs have added roughly 500 tons since early 2025. Meanwhile, lower interest rates could add further support, as Goldman expects the Fed to cut rates by another 50 basis points in 2026.

Goldman is not the only firm. Other Wall Street banks also see gold prices rising through 2026 amid ongoing global uncertainty.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

Goldman stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 5 Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GS price target of $947.60 implies 0.57% downside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Powell is a 'Disgrace' and 'Must Resign Quickly,' Says Bill Pulte
data.description:
Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) Director Bill Pulte pointed to falling inflation as a key reason why Fed Chair Jerome Powell should step down. Powell's term as Chair is set to end in May 2026, although his term as a Governor extends until January 2028.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"'Too Late' Jerome Powell is a disgrace, and after the below, he must resign very quickly," said Pulte, referencing a Truflation estimate showing 1.21% year-over-year inflation as of January 22.

Pulte Pushes for Powell's Resignation

The latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) update pointed to prices rising by 2.7% in December. The core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) index, which is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and excludes volatile food and energy items, increased by 2.8% in November. Both measures are still above the Fed's inflation target of 2%.

Pulte has repeatedly called for Powell's resignation, citing delayed interest rate cuts that have impacted housing affordability.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: S&P 500 Climbs as Jobless Claims Undershoot Estimates
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Thursday's trading session higher following the release of new labor market data from the Department of Labor.

For the week ended January 17, initial jobless claims rose by 1,000 to 200,000, below the consensus estimate of 208,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, decreased by 26,000 to 1.849 million, also below the expectation of 1.890 million.

data.title: Notion's IPO Could Shake the Market
data.description:
Notion, a $12 billion company that provides productivity software, plans to go public as soon as possible and raise around $200 million through the deal. At this moment, it is clear that the firm is starting to apply some strategies toward an IPO, such as expanding into other countries and securing multiple rounds of financing.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Notion's Strategies and Results

Since 2022, Notion has reached an estimated value of $10 billion, and two big companies, Sequoia and Index Ventures, have invested in the company. In addition, the software firm looks forward to establishing another office in emerging markets, such as Brazil. According to the executives, the South American country has some of the most engaged users in the world when it comes to adding AI to daily work duties. The private company's plan is for the long term, and proof of this is that they hired two executives with years of experience, Francisco Chang and Arthur Rozenblit.

Along with it, Notion has had two distinct eras. The first, Hypergrowth, refers to the period when the software company saw its valuation jump 12.5 times in just 2 years (2019 to 2021), and revenue went from $3 million to $31 million. Subsequently, the second period can be referred to as Revenue Catch-up, since it increased nearly 10 times, from $67 million to $600 million, in just 3 years (2022 to 2025), but the valuation stayed flat compared with the previous period.

Another strategy they are using is to raise multiple rounds of investments. This is common for startups, such as the fintech Ramp (RAMP), which secured four financing rounds in the last year. By the end of 2025, Notion's investors injected $200 million to accelerate the IPO process, and it seems to be just the beginning of more injections of capital.

How Should Notion be Priced?

So far, Notion's stock price is unknown. In spite of this, some investors are slightly apprehensive, since Section 409A may trigger ordinary income treatment. This legislation aims to prevent executives and employees from using compensation to push taxes into the future. Violating the rules results in immediate taxation with an extra 20% penalty for the individual. On top of that, the company may face reporting obligations, withholding requirements, and regulatory risks.

If the company sets a price higher than expected, employee morale is likely to improve, as higher valuations increase the perceived value of equity. That paper wealth can then be turned into cash through liquidity events such as secondary sales. As a point of comparison, OpenAI and Stripe have used similar strategies.

Moreover, the secondary windows are a risky step for any company, but Notion seems to be conservative at the moment. The firm's leadership is trying to minimize chaotic transactions and parallel marketing. In the meantime, investors have additional exposure without waiting for an IPO. Within this framework, the pre-IPO enables upward 409A valuation adjustments, offers early investors partial liquidity, and the market seems to be confident. With all of those items together, Notion's outlook seems to be very positive for the next steps.

Furthermore, the integration with AI provided a significant increase in Notion's revenue. After this addition, advanced users started to use these neural networks and subscribe to more expensive plans, expanding its income.

All in all, we still don't know how much Notion's stock will cost. Although there are a lot of good indicators for Notion's IPO, if it moves forward, the tax obligations will rise, but early liquidity can fund them, minimizing personal risks. Until that moment, the company's steps show fiscal discipline and make it a good company to watch this year.
data.author: Daniel Nigri Miranda

data.title: Here's Why 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Stock (SXTP) Rocketed Today
data.description:
60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals (SXTP) stock underwent a massive rally on Thursday after the specialty pharmaceutical company announced a partnership with Runway Health. This agreement will have the two work together to expand pre-departure access to ARAKODA for the prevention of malaria among international travelers. This plan will utilize Runway Health's travel medicine platform.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals and Runway Health will start their partnership on April 2, 2026. At this time, users of Runway Health's direct-to-patient telehealth platform will be able to receive ARAKODA, which is the only broad-spectrum, once-weekly prescription malaria prevention available in the U.S.

Malaria prevention is an important topic for travelers heading to countries suffering from the endemic. If contracted from the Anopheles mosquito and left untreated, malaria can progress to severe malaria, which is often fatal.

60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Stock Movement Today

60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals stock was up 168% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a slight dip yesterday. The stock has decreased 1.62% year-to-date and 88.62% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of SXTP stock, as more than 92 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 200,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Alphabet Stock (GOOGL) Wins a New Street-High Price Target Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) stock gained 1.98% on Wednesday and rose another 1.82% in early trading, helped by easing geopolitical worries. The stock also got a lift after Raymond James upgraded the shares ahead of its Q4 earnings report on Wednesday, February 4.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a new note, five-star analyst Aaron Kessler upgraded the stock's rating to Strong Buy from Outperform and raised his price target to a Street-high $400 from $315, implying 22% upside from current levels. Kessler pointed to improving trends across Alphabet's AI stack, including cloud infrastructure, AI tools, and search-related products. He believes the company is entering a phase where its AI story is gaining strength, which could support further estimate increases over time.

Looking ahead, Kessler expects AI growth to be the main driver of large internet stocks. For Alphabet, he sees AI adoption leading to higher revenue, supporting the Strong Buy rating.

What Lies Ahead for Investors?

Investors are now looking ahead to Wednesday, February 4, 2026, when the company reports its Q4 and full-year 2025 results. Wall Street analysts expect Alphabet to report earnings per share of $2.63, up 22.3% from the year-ago quarter. Meanwhile, revenues are expected to increase to $111.12 billion from $96.44 billion, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: ABT Earnings: Abbott Stock Sinks as Profit Hit Takes Shine Off Sales Growth
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories' (ABT) shares came down with a fever early Thursday after the American healthcare company posted sales growth for its fourth quarter, but saw a dip in its profit. Its earnings also fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the last three months of 2025, the Illinois-based company, which manufactures drugs, medical devices, diagnostic equipment, and nutritional products, generated $11.46 billion in sales. This is roughly a 5% rise from $10.97 billion seen a year ago, but is below the Wall Street consensus of $11.80 billion.

In addition, the firm reported diluted earnings per share of $1.01, which marks an 81% drop from $5.27 a year earlier and missed analysts' expectations of $1.50.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why Is iOThree Limited Stock (IOTR) Up Today?
data.description:
iOThree Limited (IOTR) stock rocketed higher on Thursday following an update for its V.Secure maritime cybersecurity system. According to the company, V.Secure has been granted IACS UR E27 Type Approval by RINA, which is the international classification society.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

iOThree Limited noted that this certification confirms that V.Secure meets the IACS technical cybersecurity requirements for Computer-Based Systems installed on ships. This includes access control, malicious code protection, event logging, communications integrity, recovery support, and safe integration.

Darryl See, Chief Technology Officer of iOThree Limited, stated, "Achieving RINA's E27 Type Approval marks a key milestone, affirming V.Secure's technical integrity and operational value. Designed as a simplified onboard Security Operations Center, V.Secure provides continuous cyber threat monitoring and automated response in full compliance with IACS standards, giving shipowners confidence in its performance at sea."

iOThree Limited Stock Movement Today

iOThree Limited stock was up 123.55% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 12.26% rally yesterday. The shares have also climbed 20.08% year-to-date and 91.36% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came increased trading activity for IOTR stock, as some 37 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 233,000 units.
data.author: William White
